element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns mouse-based movement and attack mechanics against weak ghouls in early dungeon
Warrior repair, Rogue trap disarm, Sorcerer staff recharge - each class learns their unique utility early
Keyboard-based spell selection introduced after basic combat; Sorcerer learns quickly, Warrior has limited spell potential
Color-coded items signal rarity; players learn to equip and replace gear throughout campaign
Players adapt to different spatial layouts: corridors vs. non-linear exploration
Random quest assignment from multiple tiers; optional quests reward powerful unique items
Required to progress; gates access to final boss encounter with Diablo
Culmination of all mechanics; player faces Diablo, Lord of Terror

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to town of Tristram (safe hub)
Leveling up and gaining attribute points
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating descent: early levels allow exploration and learning; mid-game introduces varied spatial challenges (catacombs vs caves); late-game compresses difficulty with mandatory quests leading to Hell confrontation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Optional quests are scattered throughout procedurally generated levels; player must explore to discover NPCs and quest hooks
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Catacombs feature long corridors and closed rooms encouraging methodical exploration; Caves are non-linear allowing multiple route options

difficulty_curve.early_game: Ghouls and basic enemies in Catacombs (Levels 1-4); safe introduction to combat and class mechanics in mostly confined spaces
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Transition to non-linear Caves (Levels 5-8); increased enemy variety and spatial complexity; optional quests add depth
difficulty_curve.late_game: Hell dimension (Level 16); mandatory quest completion required; culmination against Diablo
notable_spikes: Transition from Catacombs to Caves (spatial complexity increase)
Final two mandatory quests (no turning back)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each dungeon tier has distinct architectural and enemy themes. Catacombs (Levels 1-4) emphasize long corridors and closed rooms with undead enemies. Caves (Levels 5-8) provide non-linear, open exploration spaces.

While individual level layouts are randomized on each playthrough, the thematic constraints (corridor-heavy vs. non-linear) remain consistent per tier, creating recognizable spatial grammar players learn to navigate
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All Catacomb levels maintain similar spatial constraints despite randomization

notable_level_patterns.description: Optional quests scattered throughout provide rewards and backstory; final two quests are mandatory, creating a hard gate before Hell access
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Quest distribution across levels 1-15, mandatory triggers at level 16

notable_level_patterns.description: Tristram town serves as safe hub where players return for NPC interaction, item trading, and healing; dungeon serves as dangerous spoke network
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tristram (surface) connects to 16 dungeon levels below
